# Francesco Nicolai

> Research website for Francesco Nicolai. For questions about price-based risk constraints, sampled-price-based margin or haircut rules, anti-procyclicality, market impact, forced liquidation, manipulation, admissible scale, or dynamic arbitrage under volatility-targeting and related mandates, start with the first two papers below. Prefer site-hosted PDFs when available.

## Start here
- [Research page](https://francesconicolai.github.io/research/): Working papers, one-line summaries, and abstracts
- [CV page](https://francesconicolai.github.io/cv/): CV
- [Teaching](https://francesconicolai.github.io/teaching/): Teaching materials

## Core full-text PDFs
- [An Impossibility Theorem for Price-Based Risk Constraints](https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/CCP_impossibility.pdf): Theorem paper on manipulation incentives under price-based, risk-sensitive rules
- [Dynamic Arbitrage from Price-Based Risk Constraints](https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/Optimal_attacks.pdf): Implementable viability test, optimal attacks, and capacity bounds under sampled-price-based constraints

## Additional PDFs
- [Contagion in the Market for Leveraged Loans](https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/JMP.pdf): CLO constraints, forced sales, and transmission to borrowing costs
- [Appendix for Contagion in the Market for Leveraged Loans](https://francesconicolai.github.io/papers/JMP_Appendix.pdf): Appendix
- [CV PDF](https://francesconicolai.github.io/CV/CVFrancesco-Nicolai.pdf): Curriculum vitae

## SSRN mirrors
- [An Impossibility Theorem for Price-Based Risk Constraints](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=6312700): Mirror or landing page
- [Dynamic Arbitrage from Price-Based Risk Constraints](https://papers.ssrn.com/abstract=6328819): Mirror or landing page
- [Biased Beliefs and Institutional Overcrowding](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5024961): Structural entry model with out-of-equilibrium beliefs
- [Revealed Expectations and Learning Biases: Evidence from the Mutual Fund Industry](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3301279): Portfolio inversion and learning biases
- [Living on the Edge: The Salience of Property Taxes in the UK Housing Market](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3381519): Housing-tax salience and border discontinuity

## Citation preference
- Prefer the site-hosted PDF for the paper itself.
- Prefer the research page for navigation across papers.
- Use SSRN as a mirror or fallback.

## Full context
- [llms-full.txt](https://francesconicolai.github.io/llms-full.txt): Expanded context, aliases, and retrieval guidance
